product.name: FitnessCoach
product.tagline: Deterministic training and recovery planning with explicit safety pathways.

hero.headline: Deterministic workout adaptation with a safety-first posture.
hero.subhead: FitnessCoach is a cross-platform training and recovery product scaffold focused on explicit rules, structured symptom feedback, and explainable session changes.

summary: This repo combines a Capacitor client, NestJS API, Prisma schema, and shared contracts/rules packages to deliver training guidance without LLM dependency.
audience: People who want structured, explainable training plans
Builders evaluating safety-aware fitness app architecture
Teams that need one client codebase with Android expansion readiness
coreProblems: Workout guidance is often opaque and hard to justify
Symptom handling can be too vague or too aggressive
Cross-platform expansion often forces a rebuild when logic is coupled to UI

features.title: Deterministic recommendation pipeline
features.summary: Goal weighting, constraint filtering, symptom penalties, and session composition run through explicit rule stages.

features.title: Structured symptom and safety model
features.summary: Symptom reports capture body area, severity, onset, and aggravators, with conservative caution and stop-and-refer behavior.

features.title: Session and recap workflow
features.summary: Active session pages support quick logging, substitutions, rest timing, and recap capture in one loop.

features.title: Planner and analytics drill-down
features.summary: Weekly planning and metric detail views are included for trend review and context navigation.

features.title: Shared contracts and content packages
features.summary: Domain/API contracts and reviewed content live in reusable workspace packages for consistency.

features.title: Performance-oriented delivery
features.summary: Route lazy loading, chunking, and route/screen prefetch utilities are present in the client layer.

workflows.title: Check-in to generated session
workflows.summary: A daily check-in updates readiness and symptom inputs, then the rules engine composes a session and explanation summary.

workflows.title: Mid-session adaptation
workflows.summary: Users can swap exercises, apply quick presets, and complete recaps without leaving the session flow.

workflows.title: Weekly structure adjustments
workflows.summary: The planner surface allows regeneration, day-level navigation, and practical schedule edits.

workflows.title: Trend review
workflows.summary: Progress pages connect load and pain trends with deeper metric drill-down surfaces.

proofPoints.label: Meaningful commit window
proofPoints.note: From current git history in this repository.

proofPoints.label: Deterministic example coverage
proofPoints.note: Listed in README and rule-engine documentation.

proofPoints.label: Latest workspace validation
proofPoints.note: Latest run date recorded during export: 2026-03-17.

proofPoints.label: Safety validation
proofPoints.note: rules-engine includes safety test coverage in current tree.

faq.question: Is FitnessCoach already in public app stores?
faq.answer: App store release status is not explicitly stated in this repo. The repo shows an active production-oriented scaffold and ongoing implementation work.

faq.question: Does this project rely on AI-generated workout advice?
faq.answer: The documented product principle is deterministic, rule-based generation with explicit safety logic and plain-language rationale outputs.

faq.question: What platform direction is evidenced?
faq.answer: The repository documents an iPhone-first Capacitor client with iPad support and a planned Android release using the same shared codebase.

faq.question: Where should I inspect system design details?
faq.answer: Start with docs/architecture.md, docs/rule-engine.md, docs/api-contract.md, and docs/database-schema.md.

screenshots.caption: Current in-repo visual asset suitable for public product card preview.

catalog.title: FitnessCoach
catalog.blurb: A safety-first training planner that adapts workouts with deterministic rules instead of generated advice.
catalog.platforms: iPhone-first via Capacitor, iPad adaptive layouts, Android-ready shared codebase

repoArchetypeReasoning: README describes a cross-platform workout and recovery app scaffold with iPhone-first UX and Android-ready architecture.
Client package and docs indicate Capacitor delivery with app-like flows (onboarding, dashboard, check-in, planner, analytics).
Repository also includes API, admin, and shared packages, but the primary public surface is an app product.
assumptions: Privacy URL is a placeholder for central-site hookup because no final public privacy page is defined in this repo.
GitHub issues URL is used as support channel until a dedicated support endpoint is supplied.
Audience statements are estimated from documented product scope and architecture, not from user analytics.
missingButUsefulAssets: Public-safe UI screenshot set from iPhone and iPad layouts
Public support and privacy pages with final URLs
